Bank Marketing Campaign Dashboard
Yujin Jeong

  • Customer Segments
  • Campaign Performance
  • Engagement and History
  • Economic Context
  • Key Findings

Total Campaign Contacts

41,188

Term Deposit Subscriptions

4,640

Overall Subscription Rate

11.3%

Average Call Duration

4.3 min

This dashboard summarizes a Portuguese bank marketing campaign. The main goal is to understand which customer groups and campaign conditions are associated with a higher probability of subscribing to a term deposit. The target variable is y, where Yes means the customer subscribed and No means the customer did not subscribe.

The dashboard focuses on four questions:

  1. Which customer segments have higher subscription rates?
  2. Which campaign timing and contact methods perform better?
  3. How does call duration relate to campaign success?
  4. What economic conditions are associated with successful subscriptions?

What this means: Most contacted customers did not subscribe. Because the success rate is low, the dashboard focuses on finding segments and campaign conditions where the subscription rate is higher.

What this means: Customers aged 60+ have the highest subscription rate, while middle-age groups are lower. This suggests age is an important customer segment for the campaign story.

What this means: Students and retired customers show stronger subscription rates than several working categories. This helps identify which customer groups may respond better to term deposit offers.

What this means: Subscription performance changes by month. Months with higher rates may reflect better timing, customer readiness, or external economic conditions.

What this means: Cellular contact performs better than telephone contact. For marketing strategy, contact channel choice may affect customer response.

What this means: Customers who subscribed generally had longer calls. This is useful for understanding engagement, but it should not be used alone for targeting because duration is only known after the call.

What this means: Customers who had prior campaign contact show a much higher subscription rate. Prior engagement may indicate stronger interest or better campaign follow-up opportunities.

What this means: Subscription outcomes are associated with different Euribor rate levels. This suggests broader economic context may influence customer willingness to subscribe.

Average Economic Indicators by Campaign Outcome
Subscription Outcome Average Euribor 3M Average Number Employed Average Consumer Confidence
No 3.811 5176.2 -40.6
Yes 2.123 5095.1 -39.8

What this means: The average economic indicators differ between subscribers and non-subscribers. This supports the idea that campaign performance is connected not only to customer profile, but also to market conditions.

✨ Key Findings

These insights summarize what the dashboard shows about customer subscription behavior.

👥 Customer Segments

Older customers, especially the 60+ group, showed the strongest subscription rate. Age can help identify higher-potential campaign audiences.

💼 Job Type Differences

Subscription rates varied by job type. Students and retired customers showed stronger performance than several other occupation groups.

📅 Campaign Timing

Subscription rates changed across months, which suggests that campaign timing may influence customer response.

📱 Contact Method

Cellular contact performed better than telephone contact. The way customers are reached appears related to campaign success.

🔁 Prior Contact History

Customers with previous campaign contact showed higher subscription rates than those with no prior contact.

⏱️ Call Engagement

Subscribed customers tended to have longer call durations, although duration is only known after the call happens.

💡 Recommended Marketing Strategy

Rather than contacting every customer equally, the bank should use a more targeted campaign strategy based on customer profile, timing, contact method, and prior engagement.

🎯 Prioritize older customers, students, and retired customers.

📱 Use cellular contact when possible.

📅 Plan campaigns around stronger-performing months.

🔁 Build follow-up strategies for previously contacted customers.

🌸 Final takeaway: Campaign success is not random. Better results may come from reaching the right customers, through stronger contact channels, at better campaign times.
